title: Hanuman Dandi temple
tagline: Hanuman Dandi temple — spiritual town of Gujarat

overview.short: The Hanuman Dandi Temple is a Hindu temple located on Beyt Dwarka, an island off the coast of Gujarat, India, dedicated to the deity Hanuman and his son Makardhwaj.
overview.description: The Hanuman Dandi Temple is a Hindu temple located on Beyt Dwarka, an island off the coast of Gujarat, India, dedicated to the deity Hanuman and his son Makardhwaj. Situated approximately 5 kilometers from the Dwarkadhish Temple in Bet Dwarka, this temple is unique as it features idols of Hanuman and Makardhwaj, showcasing a rare depiction of Hanuman, who is traditionally considered a celibate, with his progeny.

topPlaces.name: Bet Dwarka
topPlaces.description: Bet Dwarka (also spelled Beyt Dwarka) or Shankhodhar is an inhabited island at the mouth of the Gulf of Kutch, situated 2 km (1 mi) off the coast of the town of Okha, Gujarat, India, and 25 km (16 mi) north of the city of Dwarka. Northeast to southwest, the island measures 8 km (5 mi) long and averaging 2 km (1 mi) wide.

topPlaces.name: Shri Keshavraiji Temple, Bet Dwarka
topPlaces.description: Shri Keshavraiji Temple is located on an island Bet Dwarka, in Gujarat, India. It is an ancient temple dedicated to Lord Krishna which was built by the Pushkarna (Pushtikar) Brahmin community.

topPlaces.name: Sudarshan Setu
topPlaces.description: The Sudarshan Setu is a cable-stayed bridge in India, connecting the Beyt Dwarka island in the Gulf of Kutch and Okha. The total length of the bridge is 2,320 metres (7,612 ft).

topPlaces.name: Okha, India
topPlaces.description: Okha is a coastal town in Devbhoomi Dwarka district of Gujarat state in India. Dwarka is situated 30 km south, and Bet Dwarka island is situated 2.9 km across a small creek from Okha port.

items.note: Bet Dwarka (also spelled Beyt Dwarka) or Shankhodhar is an inhabited island at the mouth of the Gulf of Kutch, situated 2 km (1 mi) off the coast of the town of Okha, Gujarat, India, and 25 km (16 mi) north of the city of Dwarka. Northeast to southwest, the island measures 8 km (5 mi) long and averaging 2 km (1 mi) wide.
Shri Keshavraiji Temple is located on an island Bet Dwarka, in Gujarat, India. It is an ancient temple dedicated to Lord Krishna which was built by the Pushkarna (Pushtikar) Brahmin community.
The Sudarshan Setu is a cable-stayed bridge in India, connecting the Beyt Dwarka island in the Gulf of Kutch and Okha. The total length of the bridge is 2,320 metres (7,612 ft).
Okha is a coastal town in Devbhoomi Dwarka district of Gujarat state in India. Dwarka is situated 30 km south, and Bet Dwarka island is situated 2.9 km across a small creek from Okha port.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Hanuman Dandi temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Hanuman Dandi temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Hanuman Dandi temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Jamnagar Airport (91 km) and the nearest railway station is Rajkot Junction (171 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Hanuman Dandi temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Hanuman Dandi temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Hanuman Dandi temple?
faq.a: Stays in Hanuman Dandi temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Hanuman Dandi temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Bet Dwarka, Shri Keshavraiji Temple, Bet Dwarka, Sudarshan Setu, Okha, India.
